description_long: This object is a vintage tabletop radio receiver with a rectangular wooden casing and a classic mid-century design. The front panel features a large speaker grille on the upper half, made of a slatted or perforated material, and a metallic control panel below with a prominent tuning knob, several smaller buttons, and a horizontal frequency indicator. The sides and top are plain, showcasing the wood grain, while the back panel is a flat, textured surface with ventilation holes and access ports for power and antenna connections. The overall appearance suggests a robust, well-crafted appliance typical of radios from the mid-20th century. The object is compact and boxy, suitable for placement on a shelf, table, or countertop in an urban environment. Its design emphasizes both functionality and aesthetic appeal, with tactile controls and a visible frequency display.
description: A vintage wooden tabletop radio receiver with a large speaker grille, metallic control panel, tuning knob, and frequency indicator. The boxy design features wood grain sides and a vented back panel.
description_view_0: The front view shows the speaker grille, metallic control panel, tuning knob, and frequency indicator.
description_view_1: The left side view displays a plain wooden surface with no visible controls or features.
description_view_2: The back view reveals a flat panel with ventilation holes and access ports, lacking any controls or display.
description_view_3: The right side view is similar to the left, showing a plain wooden surface.
